claims.text: Over 90 percent of Earth's animal species remain undiscovered and cataloguing them all could take another thousand years.

evidence.quote:
there's so much biodiversity that over
90 percent of animals are yet to be
discovered

claims.rationale: Mora et al. 2011 (published in PLOS Biology) famously estimated ~86% of terrestrial and ~91% of ocean species still await description - that's the likely source. The figure is for all eukaryotes not just animals, and newer methods (eDNA, metabarcoding) may accelerate description, so 'thousand years' is a loose projection. Medium directTruth.

claims.text: Life on Earth began over three billion years ago as a single cell via abiogenesis.

evidence.quote:
When life began over three billion years
ago, it was a single cell

claims.rationale: Oldest fossil evidence of single-celled life (stromatolites, Pilbara microfossils) dates to at least 3.5 billion years ago; molecular-clock analyses suggest origins up to 3.8-4.0 billion years. Abiogenesis (prebiotic chemistry → life) is the standard scientific model.

claims.text: Over nine million species of animals have been identified on Earth today.

evidence.quote:
Today, there are over nine million identified
species of animals

claims.rationale: Mora et al. estimate ~8.7 million total eukaryotic species on Earth, of which ~1.5-2 million are described. The '9 million identified animals' overstates described animals - current count is about 1.3 million described animal species. The video is likely conflating 'identified-or-estimated' with 'described'.

claims.text: The regal horned lizard shoots toxic blood almost six feet from its eye sockets as a defense mechanism, using venom sequestered from harvester ants.

evidence.quote:
This harvester ants, it's immune to the venom, but
it stores the venom and deploys it in
that bloodstream like a bioweapon

ld shoot a stream
almost six feet accurately.
And

claims.rationale: Autohemorrhaging in Phrynosoma (horned lizards) is well-documented - they squirt blood from sinuses near their eyes up to about 5 feet. The toxicity/canid-deterrence effect comes from their harvester-ant-rich diet; compounds in the blood are foul-tasting to canine predators. Core facts are solid.

claims.text: Giant isopods are eaten as food in parts of Asia.

evidence.quote:
in some parts of Asia.
They eat them

claims.rationale: Giant isopods (Bathynomus) have been served at ramen shops in Taiwan and Japanese novelty restaurants, with coverage in mainstream news (BBC, Reuters). Not a mainstream cuisine, but the claim of 'some parts of Asia eat them' is accurate.

claims.text: The star-nosed mole has 25,000 sensory receptors called Eimer's organs on its star and is the world's fastest-eating mammal.

evidence.quote:
t's
covered in about twenty five thousand scents,
receptors known as Elmers organs we acquired.
Its

The star nose mole is
the world's fastest eating mammal

claims.rationale: Eimer's organs are real tactile receptors named after Theodor Eimer. The star-nosed mole (Condylura cristata) has about 25,000 of them and holds the Guinness-recognized title as fastest-eating mammal - Kenneth Catania's lab measurements show prey identification in ~8ms and consumption under 230ms.

claims.text: The Amazonian giant centipede has caused at least one documented human death by envenomation.

evidence.quote:
At least one human death
has been attributed to its bite

claims.rationale: A 2014 case report (Toxicon, Medeiros et al.) documented the death of a 4-year-old child in Venezuela from Scolopendra gigantea envenomation. This is the singular documented case. Core fact is correct with primary-literature backing.

claims.text: Cyclopia is a real birth disorder causing a single eye that affects roughly one in 16,000 animal births including humans.

evidence.quote:
Cyclopedia is a birth disorder that actually
affects one in 16000 animal birds

claims.rationale: Cyclopia is a real, documented craniofacial malformation (severe form of holoprosencephaly). The 1:16,000 prevalence figure matches common citations for HPE spectrum overall; cyclopia specifically is rarer (~1:100,000 live births). Video slightly overstates incidence of most-severe form.

claims.text: The goblin shark is a rare deep-sea shark with a lineage extending back 125 million years and the fastest jaws of any shark.
entities: quantity:hundred and twenty five million years

evidence.quote:
the goblin shark is a rare species
of deep sea shark whose lineage goes back
a hundred and twenty five million years
It has the fastest jaws in the ocean

claims.rationale: Goblin shark (Mitsukurina owstoni) is the sole living member of family Mitsukurinidae with fossil relatives dating to the early Cretaceous (~125 Mya). Their slingshot jaw mechanism, shown in slow-motion by Nakaya et al. 2016, is among the fastest jaw protrusions documented in any fish.

claims.text: The hairy/horror frog breaks its own toe bones to push them through its skin as defensive claws, and the skin regenerates.

evidence.quote:
When this frog is grabbed or attacked, it breaks
its own toes and forces the sharp bones
through its skin to create claws

claims.rationale: Trichobatrachus robustus (hairy frog, sometimes called horror/Wolverine frog) does produce defensive bone claws by breaking through skin - Blackburn, Hanken & Jenkins 2008 (Biol Letters) documented the mechanism. Skin regeneration is presumed though less rigorously studied. Core mechanism confirmed.

claims.text: The Goliath birdeater is the world's largest spider and was named by an 18th-century naturalist who observed one eating a hummingbird.

evidence.quote:
the Goliath
birdeater is the largest spider in the world

it's called bird eater because an
18th century naturalist discovered one devouring a
hummingbird

claims.rationale: Theraphosa blondi is generally considered the largest spider by mass (up to ~170g), though huntsman has larger leg-span. The hummingbird-eating observation by Maria Sibylla Merian (1705 engraving, 17th-18th century) is the documented source of the 'bird-eater' name. Dates/details correct.

claims.text: Human botfly larvae are carrier maggots that grow and feed on human flesh after being delivered by mosquito carriers.

evidence.quote:
Bot fly larva are carrier maggots that
grow and feed on human flesh

A female fly bites a mosquito and the mosquito
bites you

claims.rationale: Dermatobia hominis (human botfly) genuinely uses phoretic transfer via mosquitoes and other flies - eggs adhere to the vector and hatch on mammalian skin via body heat. Larvae burrow into skin, form characteristic warble lesions, and feed on host tissue for ~6-10 weeks. Core parasitology is correct.

dependencies.rationale: Specific example of biodiversity already discovered and genuinely terrifying.
